title: Understanding recording consent
author: Gong
notes: Used for recording-consent variation by location and industry, and for examples of consent mechanisms such as consent pages, audio prompts, and pre-call emails.

title: Providing consent to be recorded
author: Zoom
notes: Used for the claim that Zoom prompts participants for consent when joining an already recorded meeting or when recording starts.

title: Review call recordings and transcripts
author: HubSpot
notes: Used for call recordings, transcripts, search, associated CRM records, and tracked terms in a sales/service CRM context.

title: View and understand call summary page in Conversation Intelligence app
author: Microsoft
notes: Used for the call-summary page containing action items, relevant keywords, timeline, transcript, notes, and suggested notes.

title: Protecting Personal Information: A Guide for Business
author: FTC
notes: Used for retention, access, training, and confidentiality principles for sensitive customer information.

title: Data protection and privacy settings in Gong
author: Gong
notes: Used for capture, storage, sharing, retention, redaction, deletion, exclusion lists, and privacy settings for calls, emails, transcripts, and metadata.

title: How to Grow Your AI Search Visibility With LinkedIn Content
author: LinkedIn
notes: Used only for platform-adjacent guidance on using customer questions, clear structure, terminology, and evidence-backed points of view in LinkedIn content.

title: How To Turn Employee Expertise Into Useful LinkedIn Posts
author: Jeffery Schroeder
notes: Used as internal-link context only. This post is narrower and should not duplicate that broader capture-to-post workflow.
